Scraping Logic: Standard Lever API. HTTP GET request. Data Path: Root level [array]. Field Mapping: title = 'text'; location = 'location'; link = 'hostedUrl'.

Standard Greenhouse Public API. HTTP GET request. Data Path: Response JSON -> 'jobs' [array]. Field Mapping: title = 'title'; location = 'location.name'; link = 'absolute_url'; updated_at = 'updated_at'.

"Standard Ashby Public API (GET). " "Data Path: root -> 'jobs' [array]. " "Field Mapping: title = 'title'; location = 'location'; link = 'jobUrl'. " "Metadata available: 'publishedAt' for recency sorting."

"Official Teamtailor API (v1). GET request with query-param auth. " "Data Path: json -> 'data' [array]. " "Field Mapping: title = 'attributes.title'; " "link = 'links.careersite-job-url'; " "location = 'attributes.remote-status' (or include locations in query). " "Posted Date: 'attributes.created-at' (Good for sorting).

"Gatsby Static JSON. Logic: Iterate keys starting with 'career.job.jobsInfo.'. " "Key structure is [prefix].[slug].[field]. Group by [slug]. " "Field Mapping: title = '.title'; location = '.location'; department = '.department'. " "Filter: Include if 'Hong Kong' in location string."
Type: REST API. Logic: Must use the finder parameter. Finder Syntax: finder=findReqs;siteNumber=CX_3002,sortBy=POSTING_DATES_DESC Pagination: Use limit and offset inside the query string.

API Endpoint: Refer to scraping logic for full Example
Scraping Logic: import requests import time from bs4 import BeautifulSoup def scrape_barclays_master(): session = requests.Session() url = "https://search.jobs.barclays/search-jobs/results" base_domain = "https://search.jobs.barclays" all_jobs = [] current_page = 1 # Start at page 1 headers = { "User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0", "X-Requested-With": "XMLHttpRequest", "Accept": "application/json" } # --- START OF PASTE BLOCK --- while True: # Every time the loop runs, we update the CurrentPage params = { "CurrentPage": current_page, "RecordsPerPage": 16, "SearchType": 3, "FacetTerm": "1819730", # Hong Kong ID "FacetType": 2, "FacetFilters[0].ID": "1819730", "FacetFilters[0].FacetType": 2, "FacetFilters[0].Display": "Hong Kong China", "FacetFilters[0].IsApplied": "true", "OrganizationIds": "13015", "SearchResultsModuleName": "Search Results" } print(f"📡 Fetching Page {current_page}...") try: response = session.get(url, params=params, headers=headers) data = response.json() # 1. Extract the HTML from the JSON results key html_content = data.get("results", "") if not html_content.strip(): # Check if it's empty or just whitespace print("🏁 No more jobs found (Empty results). Ending scrape.") break # 2. Parse that specific HTML string soup = BeautifulSoup(html_content, "html.parser") batch = soup.select("a[data-job-id]") # Target the job links if not batch: print("🏁 Page returned but contained no job cards. Ending.") break # 3. Process the batch for link in batch: title_tag = link.find(["h2", "h3"]) title = title_tag.get_text(strip=True) if title_tag else link.get_text(strip=True) # RECONSTRUCTION: Always prepend the domain to relative links full_url = base_domain + link['href'] all_jobs.append({"title": title, "url": full_url}) print(f"✅ Page {current_page} success: Added {len(batch)} jobs.") # 4. Preparation for the next lap current_page += 1 time.sleep(1.5) # Anti-bot delay except Exception as e: print(f"❌ Error on page {current_page}: {e}") break # --- END OF PASTE BLOCK --- print(f"\n✨ Scrape Complete! Total jobs collected: {len(all_jobs)}") return all_jobs # Run the master script scrape_barclays_master()

HTTP GET request. Pagination: Append '&start=[offset]' to endpoint, incrementing by 10 per page until 'positions' array is empty. Data Path: Response JSON -> 'positions' [array]. Field Mapping: title = 'name'; location = 'location'; link = 'canonicalPositionUrl'; unique_id = 'id'.
Type: HTML Parsing (DOM). Selector: table.small-table tbody tr. Logic: Find all rows; first td contains title/link, second td contains date. Link Note: Links are relative (must prepend base domain).

Scraping Logic: This is a full example:# HKEX import requests import time def scrape_hkex_fixed_loop(): session = requests.Session() base_url = "https://hkex.wd3.myworkdayjobs.com/HKEXCareerPage" api_url = "https://hkex.wd3.myworkdayjobs.com/wday/cxs/hkex/HKEXCareerPage/jobs" session.get(base_url) csrf_token = session.cookies.get("CALYPSO_CSRF_TOKEN") all_jobs = [] limit = 20 offset = 0 total_available = None # Start as None to signal we haven't found it yet headers = { "accept": "application/json", "content-type": "application/json", "user-agent": "Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36", "x-calypso-csrf-token": csrf_token } # We use a True loop and break manually based on our locked-in total while True: payload = {"appliedFacets": {}, "limit": limit, "offset": offset, "searchText": ""} try: response = session.post(api_url, json=payload, headers=headers) response.raise_for_status() data = response.json() # --- THE FIX: Only set total_available once --- if total_available is None: total_available = data.get("total", 0) print(f"🎯 Target Acquired: {total_available} jobs found.") batch = data.get("jobPostings", []) if not batch: print("📭 No more jobs returned in this batch.") break all_jobs.extend(batch) print(f"✅ Progress: {len(all_jobs)} / {total_available} (Offset {offset})") # --- BREAK CONDITION --- if len(all_jobs) >= total_available: break offset += limit time.sleep(1.5) except Exception as e: print(f"❌ Error: {e}") break print(f"\n✨ Final Count: {len(all_jobs)} jobs.") return all_jobs # Run the full scrape scrape_hkex_fixed_loop()
